name: From Pedrera to Sierra de Yeguas – circular by Martín de la Jara
name_original: Desde Pedrera a Sierra de Yeguas – circular por Martín de la Jara
region_intro: Road cycling around Pedrera offers diverse landscapes for exploration. The region features natural surroundings including the Sierra de la Cruz and the Higuera Riverbank, providing varied terrain. Roads and trails around the La Pedrera Reservoir present mostly well-paved surfaces, set against rugged natural landscapes, rocky hills, and dry valleys. The area includes pine forests and fragrant groves, with routes ranging from moderate to those with significant elevation gain. Best road cycling routes around Pedrera The most popular road cycling route is From Pedrera to Sierra de Yeguas – circular by Martín de la Jara, a 28.0 miles (45.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a gentle introduction to the region's rural roads. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Vicaría Observatory – Fuente de Piedra loop from Pedrera, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.3 km) path. This route features scenic views and a mix of flat sections and rolling hills. Local road cyclists also love the Martín de la Jara – Martín de la Jara loop from Pedrera, a 53.2 miles (85.6 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and small towns, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Pedrera is defined by the Sierra de la Cruz, the Higuera Riverbank, and the varied terrain surrounding the La Pedrera Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Pedrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pedrera?
A: There are over a dozen dedicated road cycling routes around Pedrera, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Pedrera?
A: The Pedrera region offers diverse landscapes. You can expect to cycle through the natural surroundings of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de la Cruz&lt;/strong&gt; and along the &lt;strong&gt;Higuera Riverbank&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes around the &lt;strong&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; feature rugged natural landscapes, distinctive rocky hills, dry valleys, pine forests, and fragrant groves of lemons, oranges, and almonds, often with views of its vibrant turquoise waters.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pedrera?
A: Yes, Pedrera offers routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995610801/from-pedrera-to-sierra-de-yeguas-circular-by-martin-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Pedrera to Sierra de Yeguas – circular by Martín de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 28.0-mile (45.1 km) trail that provides a gentle introduction to the region's rural roads.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Pedrera?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are moderate, some routes in Pedrera involve significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Osuna – Martín de la Jara loop from Pedrera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 1,300 meters of elevation gain, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pedrera?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Pedrera are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10298131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vicaría Observatory – Fuente de Piedra loop from Pedrera&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 43.7-mile (70.3 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5287189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín de la Jara – Martín de la Jara loop from Pedrera&lt;/a&gt;, a 53.2-mile (85.6 km) trail through agricultural landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can visit along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several interesting attractions. Near Pedrera, you might pass by historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estepa Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6036466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar and Convent of Santa Clara on San Cristóbal Hill, Estepa&lt;/a&gt;. Nature lovers might appreciate the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6037136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gosque Lagoon Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills Sierra de los Caballos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling in the Pedrera region?
A: Yes, Pedrera offers several long-distance road cycling options. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5287327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrera – Estepa Gate loop from Pedrera&lt;/a&gt; cover over 111 kilometers (69 miles), providing an excellent challenge for those looking for longer rides.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Pedrera?
A: The road cycling routes in Pedrera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from gentle rural roads to more challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the region's natural landscapes.

Q: Is Pedrera a good destination for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While some routes involve significant distances and elevation, there are easier routes that can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The region's natural beauty and varied terrain offer pleasant experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pedrera?
A: The region's climate generally allows for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Winter can also be suitable, with milder conditions compared to many other regions.

Q: Are there other outdoor activities available in Pedrera besides road cycling?
A: Yes, Pedrera is a hub for various outdoor activities. Beyond cycling, you can enjoy hiking and walking on numerous nature trails, especially around the La Pedrera Reservoir. The reservoir also offers water sports like kayaking and canoeing, and opportunities for swimming in the shallows. Other activities include quad biking and horse riding treks.

Q: How accessible are the road cycling routes in Pedrera by public transport or car parking?
A: While specific public transport details vary, the town of Pedrera is generally accessible. For those arriving by car, parking is typically available in and around the town, serving as convenient starting points for many routes. The region's infrastructure supports access to its outdoor activity areas.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through agricultural landscapes or small towns?
A: Yes, many routes in Pedrera traverse picturesque agricultural landscapes and connect small towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5287189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín de la Jara – Martín de la Jara loop from Pedrera&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you through such characteristic scenery.
tips: On the parallel Via Verde de las Lagunas you will find this "singular" holm oak. Holm oak cataloged as a singular tree in the municipality of Roda de Andalucía, its crown size of 23 meters stands out, it is approximately 150 years old.

2 km northeast of the municipality is a beautiful lagoon - Laguna del Gosque declared a nature reserve. Thanks to the proximity of the Laguna de Fuente de Piedra, the presence of flamingos and mallards is common.
In this locality there are some important and productive quarries from which the name of the municipality comes, since 'pedrera' is synonymous with 'quarry'.
